comment.body:
We can see this in SPX/LTY correlation, it tanked very negative. Equity risk appetite tends to recover, compressing VIX and another volatility measures.
Short-End Buybacks (Tue) and Long-End Buybacks (Thu) create dampened volatility initially. Futures First Notice Day (Fri) thins the front-month 10-yr futures (“ZN”), sparking basis squeezes and spikes in VIX/VXST.
comment.raw_body:
We can see this in SPX/LTY correlation, it tanked very negative. Equity risk appetite tends to recover, compressing VIX and another volatility measures.
Short-End Buybacks (Tue) and Long-End Buybacks (Thu) create dampened volatility initially. Futures First Notice Day (Fri) thins the front-month 10-yr futures (“ZN”), sparking basis squeezes and spikes in VIX/VXST.

comment.body: What is FND in the post?
comment.raw_body: What is FND in the post?

comment.body: A first notice day (FND) is the deadline date after which an investor who has purchased a futures contract may be required to take physical delivery of the contract's underlying commodity. The first notice day can vary by contract and will also depend on exchange rules.
comment.raw_body: A first notice day (FND) is the deadline date after which an investor who has purchased a futures contract may be required to take physical delivery of the contract's underlying commodity. The first notice day can vary by contract and will also depend on exchange rules.

comment.body:
Seeing if i understand the looming liquidity risks. Been trying to wrap my head around it for a few hours.

- TGA is at about 500B (treasury account to fund the gov...500B is is no issue)
- RRP is at about 100B (significantly down which means MMF will look to T-Bills).
- This week we have a larger than usual auction with ~500B in offerings (total). Relatively i think its a small increase overall and is likely being used to front run EOQ interest payments in June.
- TGA continues to see outflows of 100B/month, which is leading to liquidity into the markets via tax refunds, bond repayments, social services etc etc.
- TGA will hit dangerous levels around August where default risks would come up if the spending doesn't get under control (Trump tax bill passing woukd be an incredibly bullish event despite its longer term implications as it would allow for TGA to not have to disrupt market operations for funding). 

So the general understanding that i have is that if the Treasury doesn't find a way to cut spending soon (like 2 weeks lol) AND the tax bill fails the senate then it has to start over-supplying the front end of the yield curve in order to fund itself which will push the whole thing higher and since the RRP is at 100B it will cause liqudiity to be pulled from markets. Its also why Powell actually can't cut rates until the TGA inflow/outflows stabilize. The major difference between 2023 and today is that in 2023 the RRP was at 2.2T so it absorbed the heavy issuance
comment.raw_body:
Seeing if i understand the looming liquidity risks. Been trying to wrap my head around it for a few hours.

- TGA is at about 500B (treasury account to fund the gov...500B is is no issue)
- RRP is at about 100B (significantly down which means MMF will look to T-Bills).
- This week we have a larger than usual auction with ~500B in offerings (total). Relatively i think its a small increase overall and is likely being used to front run EOQ interest payments in June.
- TGA continues to see outflows of 100B/month, which is leading to liquidity into the markets via tax refunds, bond repayments, social services etc etc.
- TGA will hit dangerous levels around August where default risks would come up if the spending doesn't get under control (Trump tax bill passing woukd be an incredibly bullish event despite its longer term implications as it would allow for TGA to not have to disrupt market operations for funding). 

So the general understanding that i have is that if the Treasury doesn't find a way to cut spending soon (like 2 weeks lol) AND the tax bill fails the senate then it has to start over-supplying the front end of the yield curve in order to fund itself which will push the whole thing higher and since the RRP is at 100B it will cause liqudiity to be pulled from markets. Its also why Powell actually can't cut rates until the TGA inflow/outflows stabilize. The major difference between 2023 and today is that in 2023 the RRP was at 2.2T so it absorbed the heavy issuance
